subject: Computer Networking
title: DNS Security
description: A quick, practical guide to DNS Security.

hook.text: DNS is the phone book of the internet. Poison the phone book and you can redirect anyone anywhere.

buildup.text: DNS cache poisoning injects fake records into a resolver. Users get sent to malicious sites without knowing.

discovery.text: DNSSEC adds cryptographic signatures to DNS records. Resolvers can verify answers haven't been tampered with.

twist.text: DNS queries also leak your browsing habits. DNS-over-HTTPS encrypts them so only the resolver sees your lookups.

climax.text: Security-focused DNS providers like 1.1.1.1 and 9.9.9.9 block known malicious domains automatically.

punchline.text: Secure DNS means trustworthy answers and private questions.

quiz.question: What does DNSSEC protect against?
options: Tampered DNS records and cache poisoning
